ExpandedDescription: Search result count updates (e.g. "Showing 1-10 of 50") are not in aria-live regions, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages. Screen reader users miss when results refresh after filtering or searching.
AcceptanceCriteria: Search result count updates are announced immediately to screen readers using aria-live="polite".
Test: Perform a search or filter in litmus widget; confirm screen reader announces updated count instantly.
Solution1: Add aria-live="polite" to result count div in core-overrides
Solution2: CSS var for live region styling
Solution3: a11y.scss rule enforcing live on search results
Solution4: Theme script for dynamic result announcements

ExpandedDescription: Rating stars or thumbs in knowledge articles lack aria-label or role, violating WCAG 4.1.2. Screen readers announce only "star" without context like "Rate this article 4 out of 5".
AcceptanceCriteria: All rating components have descriptive aria-labels and roles.
Test: Interact with rating in knowledge article via screen reader in litmus; confirm full context announcement.
Solution1: Add aria-label="Rate this article" to rating elements in core
Solution2: CSS rule for star styling with ARIA

ExpandedDescription: Shopping cart items in service catalog do not announce added/removed items with aria-live, violating WCAG 4.1.3. Users miss cart status changes.
AcceptanceCriteria: Cart changes announce "Item added to cart: Laptop - Quantity 1" etc.
Test: Add item to cart in litmus; confirm announcement.
Solution1: Add aria-live to cart badge and status in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: System notifications and toast messages lack live regions, violating WCAG 4.1.3. Screen reader users miss important alerts like "Ticket submitted successfully".
AcceptanceCriteria: All notifications are announced via aria-live="assertive".
Test: Trigger notification in litmus; confirm immediate announcement.
Solution1: Add aria-live="assertive" to notification container in core
Solution3: a11y.scss rule for all .alert and toast

SpecificIssue: Screen reader reads raw HTML in widgets
ExpandedDescription: Custom widgets contain raw HTML comments or script tags that screen readers vocalize, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and causing garbled output.
AcceptanceCriteria: No raw HTML or comments are read by screen readers (hidden properly).
Test: Inspect widget with screen reader in litmus; confirm clean output only.
Solution1: Add aria-hidden or comment removal in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: Text inputs in forms lack associated <label>, violating WCAG 3.3.2 and 1.3.1. Screen readers and low-vision users have no context for fields like "Incident number".
AcceptanceCriteria: Every text input has a persistent visible label correctly associated.
Test: Focus any text input in litmus with screen reader; confirm label is announced.
Solution1: Add explicit <label for="id"> in widget templates in core

ExpandedDescription: Select dropdowns lack labels or use placeholder only, violating WCAG 3.3.2. Users don't know what the dropdown selects (e.g. "Category").
AcceptanceCriteria: Every select has a visible label and aria-labelledby.
Test: Focus select in litmus; confirm label announcement.
Solution1: Add label and aria-labelledby in core-overrides templates

ExpandedDescription: Checkbox groups lack <fieldset> and <legend>, violating WCAG 1.3.1 and 3.3.2. Screen readers treat them as unrelated items without group context.
AcceptanceCriteria: All checkbox groups wrapped in fieldset with legend.
Test: Tab through checkbox group in litmus; confirm group context announced.
Solution1: Add fieldset/legend in form templates in core

ExpandedDescription: Radio button groups lack proper grouping and labels, violating WCAG 1.3.1. Users cannot understand the group purpose.
AcceptanceCriteria: Radio groups have fieldset/legend and individual labels.
Test: Tab through radio group in litmus; confirm clear grouping.
Solution1: Add fieldset for radios in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: Buttons, links, and form controls have touch targets smaller than 44x44px, violating WCAG 2.5.5 Target Size (AAA). Mobile users with motor impairments struggle to tap accurately.
AcceptanceCriteria: All interactive elements have minimum 44px touch target.
Test: Inspect buttons/inputs in litmus on mobile emulator; measure and confirm >=44px.
Solution1: Add min-height: 44px; padding to all buttons/inputs in core-overrides
